73 /home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md
---
# Deferred Work

Items pulled out of review/implementation to preserve scope, tracked here so they don't get lost.

## Deferred from: code review of 1-1-audit-and-harden-main-branch-protection (2026-04-20)

- **Restore snippet covers ruleset-edit recovery but not ruleset-deletion** — `docs/RELEASING.md:74-80` shows a `PUT` against an existing ruleset id. If the ruleset is deleted, the restore requires `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets` with the full ruleset body (including `name`, `target`, `enforcement`, `conditions`). Story 4.1 owns the full rollback playbook and is the natural home for this expansion.

## Deferred from: code review of 1-2-create-release-github-environment-with-required-reviewer (2026-04-20)

- **Hard-coded ruleset id `13855503` and environment id `14347249917` lack a re-derive path** — `docs/RELEASING.md:14,69`. If either resource is deleted and recreated the id changes and every hard-coded URL 404s. Swap id references for name-based lookups (`gh api repos/.../rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="Default") | .id'`). Cross-cuts Story 4.1 rollback playbook.
- **"Zero env secrets" invariant has no CI assertion** — `docs/RELEASING.md:77,82`. Doc says "re-audit"; a pre-release CI check calling `/environments/release/secrets` and failing on `total_count > 0` would turn prose into an enforced gate. Belongs with the `release.yaml` workflow (Story 3.1).
- **Required-status-checks list drifts silently if `quality.yaml` job names or matrix values change** — `docs/RELEASING.md:26-34`. Doc warns itself ("update both in the same PR") but nothing enforces it; a pre-commit or CI step could diff the ruleset's check list against the workflow's job/matrix keys. Tooling improvement outside Epic 1 scope.
- **`prevent_self_review: false` → `true` flip on second-reviewer onboarding relies on prose memory** — `docs/RELEASING.md:80`. No reminder fires when a second reviewer is added. Low probability until solo-maintainer changes; revisit with team-growth.
- **`gh auth` scopes and `jq` dependency are unstated preconditions** — `docs/RELEASING.md:47-65,126-140`. A maintainer with a read-only token sees opaque 403s; `jq` absence on a fresh macOS machine breaks the restore block. Prepend a "Preconditions" subsection in a later polish pass.
- **Baseline.json capture procedure unspecified** — `docs/RELEASING.md:52-65`. Restore is aspirational if no baseline has been captured; needs a companion capture command and guidance on where baselines live. Story 4.1 rollback playbook is the natural home.
- **Site-nav discoverability of `docs/RELEASING.md` (Starlight/Astro sidebar) unverified** — `docs/RELEASING.md:1-4`. Frontmatter is Starlight-compatible but no sidebar entry was audited; if the docs site is built with Starlight, readers arriving at the site may not find the page without a direct URL. Confirm docs-site build config before acting.

## Deferred from: code review of 1-3-register-npm-trusted-publisher-for-release-yaml-workflow (2026-04-20)

- **Story 3.3 precondition — neutralize `publish.yaml` tag-push trigger atomically with `release.yaml` merge to main** — `.github/workflows/publish.yaml:10,37` has `id-token: write` and `npm publish --provenance` with `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` wired. Until Story 6.1 deletes it, a stray `v*` tag push will fire `publish.yaml` alongside (or instead of) `release.yaml`, producing publishes attributed to the wrong workflow in the provenance record — an audit-trail inconsistency, not a hard failure (token auth bypasses the TP matcher). When Story 3.3 merges `release.yaml` to main, in the same PR remove `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger (leave `workflow_dispatch` only as an emergency hatch) so the release path is single-rooted. Resolved as defer in Story 1.3 code review D2 to avoid scope creep into a doc-only story.
- **Story 3.1 precondition — workflow filename MUST be `release.yaml` (not `.yml`)** — the npm Trusted Publisher registered 2026-04-20 binds on filename `release.yaml` exactly. A `.yml` typo in Story 3.1 produces an opaque 404 on Story 3.2's alpha cut with no recovery path short of deleting-and-re-adding the TP. Re-read `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` before authoring the workflow. Enforcement is prose-only by design (Story 1.3 code review D1, resolved as defer): a stub file on main was rejected as overkill for a low-probability risk that is triple-guarded (story Dev Notes, RELEASING.md rename-coupling note, Story 3.2 alpha cut as live validator).
- **Solo-maintainer self-review weakness inherited by TP section** — `docs/RELEASING.md § Release Environment`. New `## npm Trusted Publisher` section relies on the "required reviewer" gate, which the adjacent Release Environment section admits is self-approved (`prevent_self_review: false`). The TP section does not re-acknowledge this — an auditor reading only the new section will overestimate the control. Belongs with the Story 1.2 self-review gap (already captured there).
- **Rollback Playbook heading is empty / TOC lands on no content** — `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook`. Bare `## Rollback Playbook` heading follows the `<!-- Rollback Playbook — added in Story 4.1 -->` placeholder. Readers hitting the TOC click into an empty section until Story 4.1 lands. Story 4.1's natural territory.
- **`NPM_TOKEN` rotation/deletion before Story 6.3 has no documented fallback** — `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher`. Section names the token as "last-resort token-based publish" if OIDC breaks, but does not cover the inverse failure: token rotated/revoked during an OIDC-broken window leaves both paths down. Incident-response design belongs in Story 6.3's removal plan.
- **No CI/test asserts the TP registration's shape matches repo reality** — docs-only change; no guard. Drift between the npm-side TP and the `release.yaml`/`release` env it binds to is only caught at publish time (Story 3.2 onward). A CI job that reads the RELEASING.md table and verifies (a) `.github/workflows/release.yaml` exists, (b) environment `release` exists, (c) `package.json` name matches, would turn prose into an enforced gate. Standalone story once Story 3.x lands.

## Deferred from: code review of 2-1-add-conventional-changelog-cli-as-a-devdependency (2026-04-20)

- **CI-log deprecation-warning noise persists until Story 3.1 tooling decision** — `.github/workflows/*.yaml` via `npm ci`. Every CI run prints `npm warn deprecated conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0: This package is no longer maintained.` Not a failure, but permanent log noise until the "Story 3.1 tooling decision" (below) resolves one way or the other. Acknowledged so future reviewers don't miss other deprecation notices amongst the repeated line.

content.text: Triage complete. Now writing findings to the story file and deferred-work.md.

input.old_string:
**Deferred to formal review:**

- Blind Hunter lens on OIDC surface (attestation payload shape at `.dist.attestations`, TP binding drift risk)
- Edge Case Hunter on version-string substring collisions (e.g., a future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain — Story 3.1 code-review patch D didn't flag this; worth a second look)
- Acceptance Auditor line-by-line AC #1–#14 against Debug Log References evidence
input.new_string:
**Deferred to formal review:**

- Blind Hunter lens on OIDC surface (attestation payload shape at `.dist.attestations`, TP binding drift risk)
- Edge Case Hunter on version-string substring collisions (e.g., a future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain — Story 3.1 code-review patch D didn't flag this; worth a second look)
- Acceptance Auditor line-by-line AC #1–#14 against Debug Log References evidence

**Formal code review (2026-04-21) — parallel adversarial layers (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor):**

Scope: `34ad130..HEAD` on `.github/workflows/release.yaml` only (4 load-bearing defect-fixes + net-zero temp-trigger pair). Acceptance Auditor PASS across AC #3, #5, #8, #11, #12; remaining ACs are execution-time and out-of-diff.

- [ ] [Review][Patch] Add npm version-floor assertion after `$GITHUB_PATH` update [.github/workflows/release.yaml:56-67] — Step name claims "Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing" but only installs and echoes via absolute path. Add a short step (or prefix to the next step) that (a) runs bare `npm --version` to confirm `$GITHUB_PATH` propagation beat the bundled-npm dir, and (b) fails if the major version is <11 (OIDC trusted publishing requires ≥11.5.1). Closes the gap between the step name's contract and its actual guarantee.
- [x] [Review][Defer] Substring-based `alpha|beta|rc` tag classification [.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123 + :184-190] — deferred, pre-existing. Spec line 739 already deferred this; Story 3.2's dry-run now mirrors the Story 3.1 pattern, doubling the blast surface. Future `1.0.0-march.1` would route to `rc`. Track with Story 3.3 or split story.
- [x] [Review][Defer] `npm ci` regenerates husky hook infrastructure on CI runners [.github/workflows/release.yaml:75 + :125] — deferred, defense-in-depth. `"prepare": "husky || exit 0"` installs `.husky/_/` wrappers during `npm ci`; the single safety net is `HUSKY: "0"`. If that env is ever dropped (matrix conversion, reusable-workflow split), `Commit version bump` fails because `.husky/commit-msg` invokes local-only `entire`. Belt-and-braces option: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call.
- [x] [Review][Defer] Empty `$VERSION` silently routes to `@latest` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123 + :184-190] — deferred, pre-existing. Neither the new dry-run case-chain nor the Story 3.1 real-publish chain guards `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]]`. A corrupt/empty `steps.version.outputs.new_version` falls through to `TAG=latest` and publishes under `latest`. Coordinated fix (both chains) belongs with the substring-collision defer.
- [x] [Review][Defer] `Commit version bump` uses non-conventional prefix `release:` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:125] — deferred, defense-in-depth. Standard conventional-commits types are `feat/fix/chore/docs/...`; `release:` is non-canonical. Harmless today under `HUSKY=0`, but doubles the failure surface if that env is dropped. Trivial rename to `chore(release):`.
- [x] [Review][Defer] `Create and push tag` pushes the git tag BEFORE `Publish to npm` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:161-181] — deferred, pre-existing from Story 3.1. OIDC auth failure after tag-push orphans `vX.Y.Z` on `origin` without an npm artifact. The step name "Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)" correctly narrows the claim — it does NOT cover OIDC failures. Belongs with Story 4.1 rollback playbook or a dedicated hardening story.
- [x] [Review][Defer] Tag commit orphaned on feature-branch deletion (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern) [.github/workflows/release.yaml:167 vs :170] — deferred, known pattern. `git push origin v$VERSION` is unconditional; `Push commit to main` is gated on `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`. On a feature-branch dispatch, the tag points to a commit that exists only on the feature branch; post-merge branch deletion may GC the commit (depending on GitHub's retention) and break `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` in later runs. Impacts rollback semantics; track with Story 3.3 or Story 4.1.

11 findings dismissed as noise, false positive, or spec-approved (full ledger available on request — includes PATH-append speculation contradicted by Acceptance Auditor's evidence of step-order mirroring, `HUSKY=0`-masks-problem architectural critique which the spec sanctions, self-hosted-runner edge cases not applicable to `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`, and `f449c67`-in-history noise already accepted per AC #11 no-amend rule).

- [x] [Review][Defer] `npm ci` regenerates husky hook infrastructure on CI runners [.github/workflows/release.yaml:75 + :125] — deferred, defense-in-depth. `"prepare": "husky || exit 0"` installs `.husky/_/` wrappers during `npm ci`; the single safety net is `HUSKY: "0"`. If that env is ever dropped (matrix conversion, reusable-workflow split), `Commit version bump` fails because `.husky/commit-msg` invokes local-only `entire`. Belt-and-braces option: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call.
- [x] [Review][Defer] Empty `$VERSION` silently routes to `@latest` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123 + :184-190] — deferred, pre-existing. Neither the new dry-run case-chain nor the Story 3.1 real-publish chain guards `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]]`. A corrupt/empty `steps.version.outputs.new_version` falls through to `TAG=latest` and publishes under `latest`. Coordinated fix (both chains) belongs with the substring-collision defer.
- [x] [Review][Defer] `Commit version bump` uses non-conventional prefix `release:` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:125] — deferred, defense-in-depth. Standard conventional-commits types are `feat/fix/chore/docs/...`; `release:` is non-canonical. Harmless today under `HUSKY=0`, but doubles the failure surface if that env is dropped. Trivial rename to `chore(release):`.
- [x] [Review][Defer] `Create and push tag` pushes the git tag BEFORE `Publish to npm` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:161-181] — deferred, pre-existing from Story 3.1. OIDC auth failure after tag-push orphans `vX.Y.Z` on `origin` without an npm artifact. The step name "Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)" correctly narrows the claim — it does NOT cover OIDC failures. Belongs with Story 4.1 rollback playbook or a dedicated hardening story.
- [x] [Review][Defer] Tag commit orphaned on feature-branch deletion (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern) [.github/workflows/release.yaml:167 vs :170] — deferred, known pattern. `git push origin v$VERSION` is unconditional; `Push commit to main` is gated on `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`. On a feature-branch dispatch, the tag points to a commit that exists only on the feature branch; post-merge branch deletion may GC the commit (depending on GitHub's retention) and break `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` in later runs. Impacts rollback semantics; track with Story 3.3 or Story 4.1.

content.content:
60	- **`## [Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified** — `CHANGELOG.md:5`. Path (i) pre-inserted the anchor for Story 3.2's first cut, but `conventional-changelog-cli`'s behavior on the SECOND release (does it preserve, replace, or duplicate the heading?) is preset-specific and unverified. Story 3.2's alpha cut is the explicit live validator for this; Story 3.2's review will surface concrete behavior and decide whether a re-insert step is needed in `release.yaml`.
61	- **Third-party actions (`softprops/action-gh-release@v2`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`) not pinned to SHA** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:55, 158`. Floating major-version tags. With `id-token: write` + `contents: write`, a compromised maintainer or compromised tag could exfiltrate. Industry best practice is SHA-pinning; not specified in the spec; consistent with `quality.yaml` and `publish.yaml` patterns elsewhere in the repo. Post-v1.0.0 hardening pass — likely Story 5.1 audit or a dedicated supply-chain story.
62	- **`npm install -g npm@latest` is unpinned — future npm major can break OIDC contract silently** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:52`. AC #4 / FR13 specifies "npm CLI ≥ 11.5.1" but the implementation pins to `latest`. Matches the research doc worked example, so consistent with the design baseline. A future `npm@12.x` with breaking changes to OIDC trusted-publishing surface would silently break the next release. Patch-grade fix is `npm@^11.5.1`. Revisit post-v1.0.0 alongside the broader supply-chain hardening pass.
63	- **Story 5.2 needs a one-time hand-bump path to start the v1.0.0 prerelease cycle** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:70-75`. Current `version_bump: alpha|beta|rc` logic uses `npm version prerelease --preid=...` which produces `0.10.1-alpha.0` from `0.10.0` — NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0`. To cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` (Story 5.2's plan), a one-time manual bump is needed first: `npm version 1.0.0-rc.0 --no-git-tag-version`, commit-and-push-to-main via PR, then dispatch `release.yaml` with `version_bump: rc` (which will increment to `1.0.0-rc.1`). Alternatively, extend `release.yaml` with `premajor-rc` choice option — but that bloats workflow surface for a one-time event. **Action for Story 5.1 (readiness audit):** decide between hand-bump procedure (documented in `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0-rc.1`) and workflow extension. Recommendation: hand-bump (lighter surface, one-time event). Tracked from Story 3.1 code review.
64	
65	## Deferred from: implementation of 3-2-validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch (2026-04-21)
66	
67	- **[HIGH] CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle is structurally broken by `conventional-changelog-cli -s` append mode** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:87-89` (`Update CHANGELOG.md` step) against `CHANGELOG.md`. Story 3.2's alpha cut (the designated live validator for this deferred-work from Story 3.1 review) produced concrete evidence: the preset prepends the new release entry at byte 0 of the infile, pushing the `# Changelog` title, Keep-a-Changelog preamble, and `## [Unreleased]` anchor DOWN below the new entry. Post-cut shape of `git show v0.10.1-alpha.0:CHANGELOG.md`: `line 1: ## [0.10.1-alpha.0]...`, `line 118: # Changelog`, `line 120: All notable changes...`, `line 122: ## [Unreleased]`, `line 124: ## [0.10.0]...`. Every subsequent release compounds the disorder. **Decision (Story 3.2 close-out, user-approved):** defer the fix to Story 3.3's merge PR (not Story 3.2 scope expansion). Story 3.3 should add a post-generation step to `release.yaml` that restores the preamble block to the top of the file after the preset runs — either via a small Python helper, a sed/awk pipeline, or switching to a generation approach that honors the `[Unreleased]` anchor as the insertion point. If Story 3.3 does NOT bundle this, the FIRST main-branch release cut will ship with the malformed shape and every release thereafter will compound it. Story 5.1 (pre-v1.0.0 audit) is the later backstop if Story 3.3 punts. Evidence: Story 3.2 Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References § AC #10 lifecycle Q1/Q2/Q3 answers.
68	- **[MED] Dev agent wrote `AC #N` in commit bodies; preset parsed `#N` as GH issue refs** — `0.10.1-alpha.0` CHANGELOG entry (orphaned commit `2a57dcbd`; also preserved in the published tarball's CHANGELOG). Four defect-fix commits (`f449c67`, `cbc7851`, `de2334b`, `cae5861`) had bodies referencing `AC #11` / `AC #12` meaning Acceptance Criteria — the conventionalcommits preset interpreted these as GitHub issue references and emitted `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` links in the CHANGELOG. The `Assert no bogus issue refs` guard correctly did NOT fire (refs are numeric; the guard targets the Story 2.2 class-of-bug of non-numeric refs). Cosmetic defect in a throwaway alpha entry. **Process fix for all future dev agents:** never write `AC #N` in commit bodies when the repo uses conventional-changelog tooling — write `AC N`, `AC-N`, or `acceptance criterion N`. Remediation options: (a) hand-correct the 0.10.1-alpha.0 CHANGELOG entry in Story 5.1's audit pass, (b) accept as historical artifact since AC #9 preserves the alpha as evidence only (no consumer will install `@alpha` intentionally pre-v1.0.0). Recommendation: (b) — low-value edit with npm-side mutability constraints (published tarball immutable).
69	- **[LOW] `f449c67` (first failed `--force` fix attempt) is dead-code in the feat/release-workflow chain** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml`. The `--force` approach was fully superseded by `cbc7851`'s side-prefix install; `f449c67`'s step body was overwritten one commit later. Preserving per Story 3.2 AC #11 "no amend / no force-push" rule — the commit chain is the audit trail of the debugging path. Story 3.3's merge PR will carry it as noise in the commit-list diff view. **Action:** none; Story 3.3 reviewer should be aware the 4 `fix(release):` commits include one superseded intermediate. Acceptable cost for audit integrity.
70	- **[LOW] GitHub-hosted runner's bundled npm (Node 22.22.2 → npm 10.9.7) falls below the trusted-publisher floor of 11.5.1** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml` `Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing` step. Side-prefix install resolves it but adds ~15s to every release run. Future optimization: when a Node 22.x release ships bundled npm ≥11.5.1 by default, conditionally skip the bootstrap step (`if: npm_version < 11.5.1`). Not blocking. Tracked as a Story 5.1 NFR1 (15-min CI target) cycle-time data point.
71	- **[INFO] Node.js 20 deprecation warnings on every run for `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`, `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`** — all repo workflows. GitHub forcing Node 24 by 2026-06-02; Node 20 removed 2026-09-16. Repo-wide upgrade scope, not Story 3.2. Two paths: rely on action maintainers to publish Node-24-compatible versions before the deadline (most will), or set `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` at workflow level now. Defer to a post-v1.0.0 maintenance story.
72	- **[INFO] `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6` cache-miss warning on every release run** — `release.yaml:54-57`. "No file matched to ... requirements.txt ... pyproject.toml ... uv.lock". Repo has Python test files (pytest via uv) but no Python lockfile, so uv's cache never fills. `npm test` (including Python tests) still passes; cache never invalidates. Low-priority maintenance cleanup — add a `pyproject.toml` + `uv.lock` in the Python test dir, or silence the warning via `setup-uv`'s cache-dependency-glob input. Defer.

## Deferred from: code review of 3-2-validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch (2026-04-21)

- **Substring-based `alpha|beta|rc` tag classification, now doubled by dry-run mirror** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123` (dry-run) + `:184-190` (real publish). The Story 3.2 fix `de2334b` added the dry-run case-chain byte-parallel to Story 3.1's publish chain — correct for AC #5 consistency, but the substring pattern (`*"alpha"*`, `*"beta"*`, `*"rc"*`) can misclassify unusual semver strings. Concrete example from the spec (line 739): a future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain. Proper fix is semver-aware parsing or anchoring to the prerelease identifier segment. Coordinated change to both chains. Track with Story 3.3 or split story.
- **Empty `$VERSION` silently routes to `@latest`** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123` (dry-run) + `:184-190` (real publish). Neither case-chain guards `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]]`. If `steps.version.outputs.new_version` is ever empty (bump step failed without aborting, corrupted `package.json` mid-job, etc.), `TAG=latest` and the package publishes to `@latest`. Coordinated `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]] && exit 1` guard belongs with the substring-collision fix above.
- **`npm ci` regenerates `.husky/_/` wrappers on CI; `HUSKY: "0"` is the sole safety net** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:75` (`Install dependencies` runs `npm ci` which fires the `"prepare": "husky || exit 0"` lifecycle) + `:125` (`Commit version bump` which would trip `.husky/commit-msg` without `HUSKY=0`). Defense-in-depth: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call, so a future regression that drops the job-level env (e.g. matrix split, reusable-workflow extraction) does not silently reintroduce the commit-msg failure on `entire` absence.
- **`Commit version bump` uses non-conventional prefix `release:`** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:125`. Standard conventional-commits types are `feat/fix/chore/docs/style/refactor/perf/test/build/ci/revert`; `release:` is non-canonical. Harmless under `HUSKY=0` (commit-msg hook skipped), but doubles the failure surface if that env is ever dropped. Trivial rename to `chore(release):` — combine with the `--no-verify` defense-in-depth change above.
- **Git tag pushed before npm publish — OIDC failure orphans the tag on `origin`** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:161-167` (`Create and push tag`) vs `:181` (`Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing`). Pre-existing from Story 3.1; Story 3.2 does not introduce but now the `--tag` derivation change advertises a dry-run gate that (correctly) does NOT cover OIDC failures. If OIDC handshake fails after tag-push, `v$VERSION` exists on GitHub without an npm artifact. Belongs with Story 4.1 rollback playbook or a dedicated hardening story (reorder to tag-after-publish, or add a compensating tag-delete step on publish failure).

## Deferred from: code review of 3-2-validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch (2026-04-21)

- **Substring-based `alpha|beta|rc` tag classification, now doubled by dry-run mirror** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123` (dry-run) + `:184-190` (real publish). The Story 3.2 fix `de2334b` added the dry-run case-chain byte-parallel to Story 3.1's publish chain — correct for AC #5 consistency, but the substring pattern (`*"alpha"*`, `*"beta"*`, `*"rc"*`) can misclassify unusual semver strings. Concrete example from the spec (line 739): a future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain. Proper fix is semver-aware parsing or anchoring to the prerelease identifier segment. Coordinated change to both chains. Track with Story 3.3 or split story.
- **Empty `$VERSION` silently routes to `@latest`** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123` (dry-run) + `:184-190` (real publish). Neither case-chain guards `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]]`. If `steps.version.outputs.new_version` is ever empty (bump step failed without aborting, corrupted `package.json` mid-job, etc.), `TAG=latest` and the package publishes to `@latest`. Coordinated `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]] && exit 1` guard belongs with the substring-collision fix above.
- **`npm ci` regenerates `.husky/_/` wrappers on CI; `HUSKY: "0"` is the sole safety net** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:75` (`Install dependencies` runs `npm ci` which fires the `"prepare": "husky || exit 0"` lifecycle) + `:125` (`Commit version bump` which would trip `.husky/commit-msg` without `HUSKY=0`). Defense-in-depth: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call, so a future regression that drops the job-level env (e.g. matrix split, reusable-workflow extraction) does not silently reintroduce the commit-msg failure on `entire` absence.
- **`Commit version bump` uses non-conventional prefix `release:`** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:125`. Standard conventional-commits types are `feat/fix/chore/docs/style/refactor/perf/test/build/ci/revert`; `release:` is non-canonical. Harmless under `HUSKY=0` (commit-msg hook skipped), but doubles the failure surface if that env is ever dropped. Trivial rename to `chore(release):` — combine with the `--no-verify` defense-in-depth change above.
- **Git tag pushed before npm publish — OIDC failure orphans the tag on `origin`** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:161-167` (`Create and push tag`) vs `:181` (`Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing`). Pre-existing from Story 3.1; Story 3.2 does not introduce but now the `--tag` derivation change advertises a dry-run gate that (correctly) does NOT cover OIDC failures. If OIDC handshake fails after tag-push, `v$VERSION` exists on GitHub without an npm artifact. Belongs with Story 4.1 rollback playbook or a dedicated hardening story (reorder to tag-after-publish, or add a compensating tag-delete step on publish failure).
- **Feature-branch alpha-cut tag commits are orphaned on branch deletion** — `.github/workflows/release.yaml:167` (unconditional `git push origin "v..."`) vs `:170` (conditional `Push commit to main` gated on `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`). On `workflow_dispatch` from a feature branch (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern), the `v0.10.1-alpha.0` tag points to a commit that exists only on the feature branch. Post-merge branch deletion may GC that commit depending on GitHub retention; `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` in later runs may fail or pick the wrong previous tag. Interacts with Story 4.1 rollback semantics and with FR / NFR around release-history lineage. Track with Story 3.3 or Story 4.1.

**Add npm version-floor assertion after the `$GITHUB_PATH` update** at `.github/workflows/release.yaml:56-67`.

The step is named `Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing` but only *installs and echoes*. The echo uses the absolute path — it does NOT prove that bare `npm` in the *next* step resolves to the upgraded binary, and nothing asserts the floor (OIDC trusted publishing requires npm ≥11.5.1). A short assertion closes the gap between the step name's contract and its actual guarantee.

### How would you like to handle this 1 `patch` finding?

**1.** Fix it automatically — I apply the patch now and check off the story-file item
**2.** Leave as an action item — the finding is already in the story file
**3.** Walk me through it — show full detail and suggested fix before deciding

HALT — reply with the number.

file.content:
# hard-exits if it's not on PATH — breaks CI's `Commit version bump`.
      HUSKY: "0"
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;

input.old_string:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global

input.new_string:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"

      - name: Verify npm version floor for OIDC trusted publishing
        # Runs in a fresh step so $GITHUB_PATH from the previous step takes effect.
        # Bare `npm` here asserts BOTH (a) PATH propagation worked (the side-prefix
        # install is actually first on PATH, not masked by the Node-bundled npm)
        # AND (b) the resolved version meets the OIDC trusted-publishing floor
        # (>=11.5.1). Fails fast before tag-push if either guarantee is absent.
        run: |
          NPM_VERSION=$(npm --version)
          echo "Resolved npm (via PATH): $NPM_VERSION"
          MIN_VERSION="11.5.1"
          if [[ "$(printf '%s\n%s\n' "$MIN_VERSION" "$NPM_VERSION" | sort -V | head -n1)" != "$MIN_VERSION" ]]; then
            echo "::error::npm $NPM_VERSION is below the OIDC trusted-publishing floor ($MIN_VERSION). Either \$GITHUB_PATH propagation failed (bare \`npm\` resolved to the Node-bundled version) or npm@latest regressed below the floor."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Install uv

name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    env:
      # Husky's documented opt-out (.husky/_/h checks this explicitly). Needed
      # because .husky/commit-msg invokes `entire` (local dev session tool) and
      # hard-exits if it's not on PATH — breaks CI's `Commit version bump`.
      HUSKY: "0"
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then TAG=alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then TAG=beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then TAG=rc
          else                                     TAG=latest
          fi
          npm publish --dry-run --tag "$TAG"

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

lines: echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"
+      - name: Verify npm version floor for OIDC trusted publishing
+        # Runs in a fresh step so $GITHUB_PATH from the previous step takes effect.
+        # Bare `npm` here asserts BOTH (a) PATH propagation worked (the side-prefix
+        # install is actually first on PATH, not masked by the Node-bundled npm)
+        # AND (b) the resolved version meets the OIDC trusted-publishing floor
+        # (>=11.5.1). Fails fast before tag-push if either guarantee is absent.
+        run: |
+          NPM_VERSION=$(npm --version)
+          echo "Resolved npm (via PATH): $NPM_VERSION"
+          MIN_VERSION="11.5.1"
+          if [[ "$(printf '%s\n%s\n' "$MIN_VERSION" "$NPM_VERSION" | sort -V | head -n1)" != "$MIN_VERSION" ]]; then

+            echo "::error::npm $NPM_VERSION is below the OIDC trusted-publishing floor ($MIN_VERSION). Either \$GITHUB_PATH propagation failed (bare \`npm\` resolved to the Node-bundled version) or npm@latest regressed below the floor."
+            exit 1
+          fi
- name: Install uv
uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6

- [x] [Review][Defer] Substring-based `alpha|beta|rc` tag classification [.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123 + :184-190] — deferred, pre-existing. Spec line 739 already deferred this; Story 3.2's dry-run now mirrors the Story 3.1 pattern, doubling the blast surface. Future `1.0.0-march.1` would route to `rc`. Track with Story 3.3 or split story.

- [x] [Review][Defer] `npm ci` regenerates husky hook infrastructure on CI runners [.github/workflows/release.yaml:75 + :125] — deferred, defense-in-depth. `"prepare": "husky || exit 0"` installs `.husky/_/` wrappers during `npm ci`; the single safety net is `HUSKY: "0"`. If that env is ever dropped (matrix conversion, reusable-workflow split), `Commit version bump` fails because `.husky/commit-msg` invokes local-only `entire`. Belt-and-braces option: add `-c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` or `--no-verify` to the `git commit` call.

- [x] [Review][Defer] Empty `$VERSION` silently routes to `@latest` [.github/workflows/release.yaml:117-123 + :184-190] — deferred, pre-existing. Neither the new dry-run case-chain nor the Story 3.1 real-publish chain guards `[[ -z "$VERSION" ]]`. A corrupt/empty `steps.version.outputs.new_version` falls through to `TAG=latest` and publishes under `latest`. Coordinated fix (both chains) belongs with the substring-collision defer.

### ✅ Code review actions complete

- **Decision-needed resolved:** 0
- **Patches handled:** 1 (applied)
- **Deferred:** 6
- **Dismissed:** 11

### Review Complete!

> **Story Status:** `done`
> **Issues Fixed:** 1 (npm version-floor assertion added to `release.yaml`)
> **Action Items Created:** 0
> **Deferred:** 6 (all routed to `deferred-work.md`)
> **Dismissed:** 11

**Files updated:**
- `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — inserted `Verify npm version floor for OIDC trusted publishing` step between `Ensure npm CLI…` and `Install uv`. Uses `sort -V` to assert bare `npm --version >= 11.5.1`; fails fast if `$GITHUB_PATH` propagation broke or `npm@latest` regressed.
- Story file → status `done`, review findings appended with patch checked off.
- `deferred-work.md` → 6 new entries under `## Deferred from: code review of 3-2-…`.

diff --git a/.github/workflows/release.yaml b/.github/workflows/release.yaml
index 9c67b9f..137a414 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/release.yaml
+++ b/.github/workflows/release.yaml
@@ -66,6 +66,21 @@ jobs:
           echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
           echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"
 
+      - name: Verify npm version floor for OIDC trusted publishing
+        # Runs in a fresh step so $GITHUB_PATH from the previous step takes effect.
+        # Bare `npm` here asserts BOTH (a) PATH propagation worked (the side-prefix
+        # install is actually first on PATH, not masked by the Node-bundled npm)
+        # AND (b) the resolved version meets the OIDC trusted-publishing floor
+        # (>=11.5.1). Fails fast before tag-push if either guarantee is absent.
+        run: |
+          NPM_VERSION=$(npm --version)
+          echo "Resolved npm (via PATH): $NPM_VERSION"
+          MIN_VERSION="11.5.1"
+          if [[ "$(printf '%s\n%s\n' "$MIN_VERSION" "$NPM_VERSION" | sort -V | head -n1)" != "$MIN_VERSION" ]]; then
+            echo "::error::npm $NPM_VERSION is below the OIDC trusted-publishing floor ($MIN_VERSION). Either \$GITHUB_PATH propagation failed (bare \`npm\` resolved to the Node-bundled version) or npm@latest regressed below the floor."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
       - name: Install uv
         uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
         with:

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): assert npm >=11.5.1 after $GITHUB_PATH update

The Ensure-npm step installs npm@latest into a side-prefix and appends
the .bin dir to $GITHUB_PATH, then echoes the upgraded version via
absolute path. That proves the install worked but NOT that bare npm in
subsequent steps resolves to it — a silent $GITHUB_PATH-propagation
failure would leave the Node-bundled npm (10.9.7 on Node 22.x, below
the OIDC trusted-publishing floor of 11.5.1) in place, and the real
publish step would fail after tag-push with an opaque auth error.

New step runs bare npm --version in a fresh step (so $GITHUB_PATH takes
effect), compares against 11.5.1 via sort -V, and emits ::error:: then
exits 1 if the floor is not met. Closes the gap between the Ensure step
name's contract and its actual guarantee. Surfaced by Story 3.2 formal
code review (Blind Hunter + Edge Case Hunter convergent finding).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
